channel.title: AI Thought Leader Talks (YouTube)
channel.description: Original long-form talks and interviews with AI thought leaders, filtered by Claude.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] "Perspectives on IA" : conf. de Yann LeCun, WinterWeek – Graduate School – Univ. Gustave Eiffel
item.author: Université Gustave Eiffel
item.description:
Université Gustave Eiffel · 126 min
Yann LeCun, 2018 Turing Award winner for Deep Learning, delivers a conference talk titled 「Perspectives on IA」 at the Université Gustave Eiffel WinterWeek (Feb 2026). He discusses the revival of predictive AI research via his startup AMI Labs and the development of a new approach based on 「world models.」 LeCun also reflects on his career from ESIEE to NYU to Meta FAIR, and his contributions to convolutional neural networks.

item.title: [Geoffrey Hinton] The AI Scenario Nobody Wants To Talk About | Geoffrey Hinton (The Diary Of A CEO)
item.author: The Lumen Project
item.description:
The Lumen Project · 27 min
Geoffrey Hinton, the 「Godfather of AI,」 appears on The Diary of a CEO to sound the alarm on AI risks he helped create. Topics include: why AI pioneers are worried, AI-powered cyber attacks, the threat of AI-designed biological viruses, autonomous weapons, elections manipulation by AI, and the possibility of superintelligent machines surpassing human control.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang GTC 2026 Full Keynote
item.author: Yahoo Finance
item.description:
Yahoo Finance · 140 min
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang delivers his full GTC 2026 keynote, covering: the acqui-hire agreement with AI inference chip designer Groq, the strategic Nebius-Meta $27B capacity deal for 2027, and the broader AI chip roadmap. The keynote is the centerpiece of Nvidia's annual developer conference.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] 黃仁勳最新訪談：AI 自駕已不是科幻，真正革命才剛開始｜Jensen Huang’s Interview: AI Self-Driving Is No Longer Science Fiction
item.author: New SciTech 新科技
item.description:
New SciTech 新科技 · 22 min
Jensen Huang discusses AI self-driving, end-to-end models, safety stacks, and robotaxi future in an interview with New SciTech. Huang frames autonomous driving not as a tech demo but as a preview of the next transportation revolution, and explains how AI reasoning changes the calculus on safety and deployment timelines.

item.title: [Channel: Dwarkesh Patel] Dylan Patel — The single biggest bottleneck to scaling AI compute
item.author: Dwarkesh Patel
item.description:
Dwarkesh Patel · 151 min
Dylan Patel, founder of SemiAnalysis, gives a deep dive into the three big bottlenecks constraining AI compute scaling: logic (compute), memory (bandwidth/capacity), and power. He walks through the economics of AI labs, hyperscalers, foundries (TSMC), and fab equipment makers (ASML). Key timestamps cover why H100 prices have risen, TSMC allocation dynamics, ASML as the #1 constraint by 2030, China's semiconductor scaling timeline, the incoming memory crunch, US power constraints, and the robotics/taiwan risk question.

item.title: [Channel: Machine Learning Street Talk] When AI Discovers the Next Transformer — Robert Lange
item.author: Machine Learning Street Talk
item.description:
Machine Learning Street Talk · 78 min
Robert Lange, founding researcher at Sakana AI, discusses Shinka Evolve — a framework combining LLMs with evolutionary algorithms for open-ended program search. Topics include: why AlphaEvolve gets stuck (needs a human to hand it the right problem), MAP-Elites quality-diversity search, UCB bandits adaptively selecting between frontier models mid-run, concrete results in circle packing and competitive programming, the AI Scientist critique (current systems produce workshop-level slop), and predictions for how scientific research will transform in 5–20 years.

item.title: [Channel: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] 133. 对谢赛宁的7小时马拉松访谈：世界模型、逃出硅谷、AMI Labs、两次拒绝Ilya、杨立昆、李飞飞和42
item.author: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.description:
Zhang Xiaojun Podcast · 406 min
In a 7-hour marathon interview, Saining Xie (NYU professor, AMI Labs co-founder with Yann LeCun) covers: why he believes Silicon Valley is trapped in the LLM paradigm, his two offers from Ilya Sutskever and why he turned both down, his research on world models and the Scaling Laws ceiling, his years at Google DeepMind and Meta FAIR, Diffusion Transformers, the meaning behind 「42」 in the interview title, and his perspective on human intelligence through the lens of the Diamond Sutra. Topics span academic research taste, the escape from Silicon Valley groupthink, and what AMI Labs is building.

item.title: [Topic: OpenAI Talks] Building AI for better healthcare — the OpenAI Podcast Ep. 14
item.author: OpenAI
item.description:
OpenAI · 31 min
OpenAI健康负责人Nate Gross与健康AI研究负责人Karan Singhal，讨论AI在医疗领域的应用策略与最新进展。内容涵盖：OpenAI如何训练模型处理敏感健康问题、与医疗机构合作的挑战与模式、在临床实践中的具体应用案例，以及开发过程中最令他们惊讶的发现。

item.title: [Topic: Google DeepMind Talks] Navigating the Future of AI with Logan Kilpatrick | Google Deepmind
item.author: Rishab in Cloud
item.description:
Rishab in Cloud · 45 min
Logan Kilpatrick from Google discusses the evolving AI development landscape, including: the transition from traditional PM roles to technical roles in AI teams, community engagement strategies for developer relations, Gemini 3.1 reasoning parameters and what they mean for developers, the evolution of RAG toward dynamic context retrieval, how AI tools are changing software engineering careers, and whether open source contributions will keep pace with AI-driven complexity.

item.title: [Topic: Meta AI Talks] Meta NeuroScientist SHOCKED Me: Scale Alone Won’t Create Consciousness!
item.author: Dr Brian Keating
item.description:
Dr Brian Keating · 51 min
Dr. David Sussillo (Meta Reality Labs, Stanford, former Google Brain) explains why scaling transformers alone cannot produce conscious AI, and why studying the brain may be the only path forward. Topics include: the RNN vs. transformer debate, the FORCE algorithm for training RNNs, Meta's EMG wristband research, Rich Sutton's Bitter Lesson, why the GPU-transformer paradigm may be hitting a ceiling, and his memoir 「Emergence」 about his path from foster care to the frontier of neuroscience and AI research.
